title: All the Microbes That Could Survive in Space
gist: Researchers find Earth microbes — including hardy bacteria from NASA clean rooms and 10 organisms identified as able to survive other planets' conditions, some human pathogens — could contaminate other worlds and threaten astronaut health.

insights.claim: NASA scientists discovered 26 new resilient bacterial species inside their own ultra-sterile clean rooms at Kennedy Space Center that survived UV sterilization, chemical scrubbing, and radiation.
insights.speaker: Kristen French
insights.so_what: Even best-in-class sterilization fails; planetary-protection is a hard, unsolved problem for the space-launch sector.
insights.quote: These microbes survived UV sterilization, chemical scrubbing, and radiation.

insights.claim: PhD candidate Tommaso Zaccaria identified 10 micro-organisms that could theoretically survive conditions on other planets, including human pathogens such as Klebsiella pneumoniae.
insights.speaker: Kristen French
insights.so_what: Forward-contamination could compromise the search for extraterrestrial life and astronaut health, raising the bar for crewed-mission biosafety.
insights.quote: 10 micro-organisms that could theoretically survive the unforgiving conditions on other planets

insights.claim: Transporting Earth microbes to other worlds risks seeding life there and confounding the search for extraterrestrial life, while also endangering astronauts whose immunity is already weakened by space travel.
insights.speaker: Kristen French
insights.so_what: A dual scientific and safety constraint that will shape mission design and sterilization-tech demand.
insights.locator: If we transport micro-organisms from Earth
